prerequisites: A Google Cloud project with the Google Drive API enabled in the Google Cloud Console.
Access to the Google Drive folder you want to sync (its shareable folder URL), supplied via the required folder_url config field.
OAuth credentials configured in the project's OAuth consent screen with the Drive scopes your sync requires, OR a service account JSON key with access to the target folder.
Definition of the streams to extract (the required streams config field), and optionally a delivery_method and start_date.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (user credentials)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as a Google user with an OAuth 2.0 client. Obtain a client ID and client secret from the Google Cloud Console, then complete the OAuth flow to get a refresh token. The connector exchanges the refresh token for access tokens to call the Drive API. Maps to the credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token secret fields.

authMethods.name: Service account
authMethods.summary: Authenticate server-to-server with a Google service account. Create the service account in Google Cloud Console (IAM & Admin > Service Accounts), add a JSON key, and grant it access to the target Drive folder (share the folder with the service account email, or use domain-wide delegation). Provide the downloaded JSON key via the credentials.service_account_info secret field.

setupSteps.title: Enable the Drive API in Google Cloud
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console, select or create a project and enable the Google Drive API for it. Both authentication methods call the Drive API v3, so the API must be enabled before credentials will work.

setupSteps.title: Create credentials (OAuth client or service account)
setupSteps.body: For OAuth: configure the OAuth consent screen with the Drive scopes your sync needs, then create an OAuth client ID to obtain a client ID and client secret. Complete the OAuth authorization flow to receive a refresh token. For a service account: go to IAM & Admin > Service Accounts, create a service account, then Keys > Add key > Create new key and choose JSON to download the key file.

setupSteps.title: Grant access to the target folder
setupSteps.body: Share the Google Drive folder you intend to sync with the authenticating identity. For service accounts, share the folder with the service account email (or configure domain-wide delegation). Copy the folder's URL for the connector's folder_url field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set credentials to either your OAuth client_id/client_secret/refresh_token or your service_account_info JSON. Set the required folder_url and streams. Optionally set delivery_method and start_date to control how data is delivered and the sync window.

sources.title: Google Drive API: About authentication and authorization

Using OAuth 2.0 for Server to Server Applications (service accounts)
